data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Instagram's infrastructure is built on a microservices architecture, allowing independent deployment and scaling of different components.
data.technical_spec: Each service is a small, well-defined piece of functionality, enabling isolation and scalability.

data.title: GraphQL API
data.description: Instagram utilizes GraphQL to provide a flexible and efficient way to fetch data from its servers.
data.technical_spec: GraphQL enables clients to request only the data they need and enables easier backend data management.

data.title: Data Storage Solutions
data.description: A combination of SQL and NoSQL databases are used for handling diverse data types and storage needs.
data.technical_spec: Relational databases for structured data; NoSQL for scalable and fast access to unstructured data.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Load balancing strategies are in place to ensure smooth user experience and availability across servers.
data.technical_spec: Distribution of incoming network traffic across multiple servers.

data.title: CDN Usage
data.description: Content Delivery Networks (CDNs) are used to deliver multimedia content efficiently to a global audience.
data.technical_spec: Caches content closer to users, reducing latency and load times.
